Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the data:
- Current: Y at (45,51) with 27 pixels, B at (48,51) with 40 pixels
- Previous: Y at (45,51) with 27 pixels, B at (48,51) with 40 pixels
Wait - SPACE didn't change anything! The board didn't change. The positions are identical before and after SPACE.
Let me reconsider. The previous output showed:
- Current: Y at (45,51), B at (48,51)
- Previous: Y at (45,51), B at (48,51)
But the right border yellow strip changed from 16 to 15 pixels. So only the HUD changed.
So SPACE did nothing this time! It seems SPACE only works when the target is directly above the avatar. Let me check:
- Before step 51: Avatar at (48,51), target at (48,51) - same position? No wait, let me re-read.
Actually looking at the history more carefully:
- Step 50: Avatar at (48,51), target at (48,51) - they were overlapping!
- Step 51 (SPACE): Avatar moved to (45,51), target stayed at (48,51)
- Step 52 (DOWN): Avatar moved to (48,51), target moved to (45,51) - they swapped
- Step 53 (SPACE): No change - avatar at (48,51), target at (45,51)
So SPACE works when target is directly above avatar (step 51), but NOT when target is below avatar (step 53).
Wait, but at step 51, the target was at (48,51) and avatar was at (48,51) - they were at the same position! Maybe SPACE "pushes" the avatar away from the target?
Let me reconsider the whole pattern:
1. When avatar and target overlap, SPACE moves avatar UP
2. When avatar moves toward target, target moves opposite (they swap)
3. Target grows when approached
Maybe the goal is to make the target grow to a certain size? Or maybe I need to overlap the target and then do something specific?
Let me try to overlap the target again by moving DOWN, then try SPACE.
Current state: Avatar at (48,51), target at (45,51)
If I move DOWN: Avatar goes to (51,51), target goes to (42,51) - they move apart
If I move UP: Avatar goes to (45,51), target goes to (51,51) - they swap
Wait, they're already 3 rows apart. Let me try UP to swap them so avatar is at (45,51) and target is at (51,51). Then DOWN would move avatar to (48,51) and target to (48,51) - overlap!
I could also try pressing SPACE while they're at the same position to see what happens.

# SPACE did nothing this time - only right border changed (16->15 pixels)
# Pattern: SPACE only works when target is above avatar (step 51)
# Current: Avatar (48,51), Target (45,51) - target is ABOVE avatar
# But SPACE didn't work... Let me check if board really didn't change

# Try UP to swap positions, then DOWN to overlap
action(['UP', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>

SPACE only works in specific conditions.", "goal_model": "Need to find win condition - maybe overlap target then SPACE?", "recent_findings": ["SPACE had no effect at step 53 - only HUD changed", "Target at (45,51) above avatar at (48,51)"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Try UP then DOWN to overlap target, then SPACE", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["ACTION7", "MOUSE", "SPACE when target above but not overlapping"]}}
